1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin web-UI constraint-system graph payload 2 3 All statements below are derived from OBSERVED oracle output: the 81 captured 4 crawl manifests under `oracle/captured_responses/` (14705 graph payloads) and 5 targeted live probing of the black-box HS binary (see QUERIES.log). No 6 tamarin-prover source was read. Where a behavior could not be pinned down 7 black-box it is marked **[GAP]**. 8 9 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 ## 1. Transport / payload family 11 12 A crawl manifest is JSON: `{base, lemmas, log, capped, manifest}` where 13 `manifest` maps a URL to `{kind, status, body}` (kind ∈ html|text|json|dot). 14 15 Graph-bearing URL families (theory index `N` replaces `#` in captures): 16 17 | URL family | kind | payload | 18 |---|---|---| 19 | `/thy/trace/N/interactive-graph-def/proof/[/...]` | `dot` | **the graph** — graphviz DOT text (`text/plain`) | 20 | `/thy/trace/N/intdot/proof/[/...]` | `html` | wrapper page embedding `` | 21 | `/thy/trace/N/main/cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` | `json` | `{html,title}`; html contains `` | 22 23 The DOT text at `interactive-graph-def` is the whole deliverable. `intdot` is a 24 fixed HTML shell (only the `` and the `dotsrc` path vary). The graph is 25 rendered client-side from the DOT by `/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js`. 26 27 **JSON graph payload** [GAP]: the binary exposes a *separate* `--with-json` 28 rendering backend (`tamarin-prover interactive --help`). No JSON rendering tool 29 is installed here and no `interactive-graph-def` body in the corpus is JSON — 30 the entire corpus is the DOT backend. JSON graph format is therefore unobserved. 31 32 **Graph options** [GAP]: query params on the graph URL 33 (`?simplification=`, `?abbreviate=`, `?level=`, `?compress=`, `?simplify=`, …) 34 produced byte-identical output; there is no per-request simplification-level 35 knob, and no CLI simplification-level flag. See §5. 36 37 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 38 ## 2. DOT document structure 39 40 Two headers occur, selected by a single rule (§4): 41 42 ### 2a. Simple header (non-clustered) 43 ``` 44 digraph "G" { 45 nodesep="0.3"; 46 ranksep="0.3"; 47 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2"]; 48 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica"]; 49 <statements…> 50 51 } 52 ``` 53 54 ### 2b. Compact header (clustered) 55 ``` 56 digraph "G" { 57 nodesep="0.8"; 58 ranksep="0.8"; 59 sep="4"; 60 splines="true"; 61 overlap="false"; 62 pack="true"; 63 packmode="cluster"; 64 concentrate="true"; 65 compound="true"; 66 remincross="true"; 67 mclimit="10"; 68 nslimit="20"; 69 nslimit1="20"; 70 ordering="out"; 71 rankdir="TB"; 72 showboxes="false"; 73 clusterrank="local"; 74 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2",margin="0.05,0.05",shape="ellipse"]; 75 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",penwidth="1.5",arrowsize="0.5",color="black",style="solid",weight="8"]; 76 <statements…> 77 78 } 79 ``` 80 81 ### Block / whitespace rule (byte-exact) 82 Every block (`digraph … {`, `subgraph … {`, anonymous `{`) is emitted as: 83 `OPEN "\n"` then each inner statement as `STMT "\n"`, then one blank line, then 84 `}`. i.e. the body always ends `…\n\n}`. The top-level digraph is followed by a 85 final `\n`. Subgraph/anonymous blocks are themselves statements of their parent 86 (so the parent supplies the `\n` after their closing `}`). There are **no** blank 87 lines between sections (header attrs / nodes / edges / subgraphs run together); 88 the only blank line in each block is the one before its own `}`. 89 An empty graph is header attrs + blank + `}` (154 bytes). 90 91 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 92 ## 3. Node & edge syntax 93 94 Only three node shapes appear in proof graphs: `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 95 96 ### 3a. Record node (a rule / graph-node instance) 97 ``` 98 n<ID>[shape="record",label="{{<p> cell|<p> cell}|{<p> cell}|{<p> cell|<p> cell}}",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="black|white",role="<Role>"]; 99 ``` 100 - Attribute order is fixed: `shape,label,fillcolor,style,fontcolor,role`. 101 - Label is a graphviz record: three top groups `{prem}|{info}|{concl}` (a group 102 may be absent/empty). Each group = cells joined by `|`. Each cell = 103 `<portid> text` (port id like `n0`, a space, then the fact text). Ports are 104 numbered per-graph; a record's own node id is allocated AFTER its port ids. 105 - Record-label text escaping: `<`→`\<`, `>`→`\>`, literal `{ } |`→`\{ \} \|`; 106 long facts are wrapped with `\l` (left-justified break) and indented with 107 ` ` runs. (The exact wrap width / term pretty-printing is **[GAP]** — it 108 is graphviz-layout / solver-term-renderer dependent; the model treats cell 109 text as pre-rendered.) 110 - `fillcolor` is a deterministic per-rule color (same rule name ⇒ same color 111 within a graph). `role="Undefined"` for plain multiset-rewriting rules; 112 otherwise the process/agent role. `fontcolor` is `black` on the light MSR 113 palette, `white` on the saturated per-role cluster palette. Exact color hash 114 is **[GAP]**; the model carries colors explicitly. 115 116 ### 3b. Ellipse node (atomic / knowledge / action / temporal node) 117 ``` 118 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse"]; 119 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse",color="<c>"]; 120 ``` 121 - Attribute order: `label,shape[,color]`. Optional `color` (`gray` for `!KU` 122 intruder-knowledge nodes, `darkblue` for action/event nodes, `gray30`, …). 123 - Ellipse text is NOT record-escaped (`<`,`>` appear literally, e.g. 124 `Session( 'I', <pk(~ltkA), pkB>, … )`). 125 126 ### 3c. Edge 127 ``` 128 n<S>[:<sport>] -> n<D>[:<dport>][<attrs>]; 129 ``` 130 Endpoints optionally carry a `:port` (record port). `<attrs>` is a 131 comma-separated attribute list, in observed key order. Frequent styles: 132 133 | attrs | meaning (inferred from context) | 134 |---|---| 135 | `color="red",style="dashed"` | intruder-deduction / `!KU` edge | 136 | `style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"` | structural premise/conclusion edge | 137 | `style="bold",weight="10.0"` | structural edge (uncolored) | 138 | `color="gray30"` | message/std edge | 139 | `color="blue3",style="dashed"` | temporal-order edge | 140 | `color="orangered2"` | (deduction variant) | 141 | `color="black",style="dashed"` | before/less-than temporal edge | 142 | `style="invis"` | ranking edge to the legend node | 143 | `color="green",style="dotted"` / `purple`/`darkorange3` dashed | other goal edges | 144 145 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 146 ## 4. Clustering / simplification (§ priority 3) 147 148 **Trigger rule (byte-verified over all 14705 graphs, 0 violations):** 149 > The COMPACT clustered rendering (§2b, with `subgraph "cluster_…"` blocks) is 150 > used **iff the graph contains at least one node with `role` ≠ `"Undefined"`.** 151 > Otherwise the SIMPLE header (§2a) is used. 152 153 Non-Undefined roles arise from process/SAPIC agents (e.g. `Initiator`, 154 `Responder`, `server`, `Tag`, `v`). Pure multiset-rewriting theories emit the 155 simple header everywhere; role-annotated theories emit the compact header on 156 every non-empty graph (empty graphs have no role nodes ⇒ simple). 157 158 ### Cluster block 159 ``` 160 subgraph "cluster_<Role>_Session_<k>" { 161 nodesep="0.6"; 162 ranksep="0.6"; 163 label="<Role>_Session_<k>"; 164 style="filled"; 165 color="#RRGGBBAA"; 166 penwidth="2"; 167 fillcolor="#RRGGBBAA"; 168 overlap="false"; 169 sep="4"; 170 n<ID>[shape="record",label="…",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="white",role="<Role>"]; 171 172 } 173 ``` 174 - Cluster name = `cluster_<clusterlabel>`; `label` = the clusterlabel 175 (`<Role>_Session_<k>`). Nodes grouped by (role, session instance). 176 - `color`/`fillcolor` are an 8-hex ARGB (alpha `4C`, ~30%) hashed from the 177 cluster; node `fillcolor` is the saturated per-role color. Exact hash **[GAP]**. 178 179 ### What "simplification" means here 180 There is no exposed simplification *level*; the corpus reflects one fixed 181 rendering pipeline. The observable "simplification" is the fixed compression the 182 server applies before rendering: intruder-knowledge (`!KU`) nodes are drawn as 183 gray ellipses; single-premise/conclusion structural chains are drawn with bold 184 weight-10 edges; role nodes are packed into per-session clusters (compact mode). 185 Reproducing the *content* of that compression from a raw constraint system 186 requires the solver and is **[GAP]** — out of scope for a rendering crate. 187 188 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 189 ## 5. Node abbreviation (§ priority 2) 190 191 When a graph is drawn, sufficiently complex sub-terms are replaced by short 192 NAMES and listed in a legend node. Observed from 113 924 legend rows. 193 194 ### 5a. Legend node 195 A `plain` node with an HTML-like label, placed in a sink-rank block: 196 ``` 197 { 198 rank="sink"; 199 n<ID>[shape="plain",label=<<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1"><TR>…row0…</TR> 200 <65 spaces><TR>…row1…</TR> 201 … 202 <65 spaces><TR>…rowN…</TR></TABLE>>]; 203 204 } 205 ``` 206 - Each row: 207 `<TR><TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP"><FONT COLOR="#000000">NAME</FONT></TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">=</TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">EXPANSION</TD></TR>` 208 (single spaces between `</TD>` and the next `<TD>`). 209 - The first `<TR>` is inline after the `<TABLE …>` tag; every continuation `<TR>` 210 is on its own line indented by **65 spaces = the byte length of the 211 `<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1">` opening 212 tag** (graphviz hang-indent of the table's children; independent of node id). 213 - EXPANSION is HTML-escaped: `<`→`<`, `>`→`>`, `&`→`&`. Single quotes 214 and `* ^ ~ $ ⊕` are literal. 215 - The legend node is wired to the rest with `style="invis"` edges (from a couple 216 of real nodes to the legend), emitted AFTER the sink block. 217 218 ### 5b. Naming scheme (byte-verified rule) 219 `NAME = <PREFIX><n>`, n = 1,2,3,… per prefix. 220 `PREFIX` = the first **two alphabetic characters, uppercased**, of the ROOT 221 symbol's *name* (one letter if the name has only one). The root symbol name is: 222 - function application `f(…)` ⇒ `f` (`sign`→SI, `senc`→SE, `hash`→HA, 223 `h`/`h1`/`h2`→H, `KDF`/`kdf1`→KD, `pk`→PK, `aenc`/`aead`→AE, `pmult`→PM, …) 224 - public constant `'c'` ⇒ `c` (`'uninitialized'`→UN, `'F_status'`→FS, 225 `'ho_req_ack'`→HO, `'no_message_state'`→NO — non-letters skipped: F,s→FS) 226 - variable `~v` / `$v` / `v` ⇒ `v` (`~AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`→AM, `~cid_N26`→CI, 227 `~gNB_UE_ID`→GN, `~mid…`→MI, `commitmsg`→CO, `StateChannel`→ST) 228 - infix operator, mapped to its function name then the letter rule: 229 `^`→exp→**EX**, `*`→mult→**MU**, `++`→union→**UN**, `⊕`→xor→**XO**. 230 231 Abbreviations NEST: an inner abbreviated term is referenced by name inside an 232 outer expansion (`KD1='KDF(EX1)'`, `MA1='mac(EX1, SI1)'`, `EX1="'g'^MU1"`, 233 `H1="h(<…, EX2, EX3, EX1, …>)"`). So naming is applied bottom-up. 234 235 Per-prefix numbering is by tamarin's canonical term order, NOT by first 236 appearance in the drawing (observed: `In(EX2)` is the first occurrence yet gets 237 `EX2`, and larger/deeper terms get the lower numbers within a prefix). The exact 238 tie-break total order is **[GAP]** (needs the solver's term ordering); the crate 239 assigns per-prefix numbers over a defined order and matches whenever that order 240 is unambiguous. 241 242 ### 5c. Selection — WHICH sub-terms get abbreviated **[RESOLVED — see REPORT2.md]** 243 244 **Rule (three necessary gates; each confirmed by a controlled black-box probe):** 245 > A sub-term `t` is abbreviated **iff** 246 > 1. `renderLen(t) ≥ 10` (Unicode-scalar length of the fully-expanded surface 247 > rendering, *including* `'…'` quotes and `~`/`$` sigils), AND 248 > 2. `occ(t) ≥ 2` (occurrences over the whole constraint system — graph node 249 > facts **and** the sequent's goals/formulas — edge-shared messages count 250 > once), AND 251 > 3. `t` is **not** a tuple `<…>`. 252 > Naming is bottom-up: nested eligible sub-terms are named first 253 > (`LO1='longarg123'`, then `H1='h(LO1)'`). 254 255 This is a rendered **length** gate, not the *size* or *occurrence* the earlier 256 note guessed at — which is why it looked context-free-yet-unexplained. It 257 cleanly settles the old counter-observations: `sign(<…,'g'^~ekR>,~ltkI)` is ≥10 258 chars (abbreviated); `KDF(z)` is 6 chars (never abbreviated, whatever its count); 259 `mac(z,SI1)` and `'g'^~lkR` are below the length/occurrence thresholds in their 260 graphs. `'g'^~lkR` vs `'g'^~lkR.1` differ because the `.1` copy reaches occ 2 in 261 the system while the other does not. 262 263 **Corpus-exactness (necessary direction):** across all **97 538** legend 264 expansions in **11 564** graphs, the minimum rendered length is exactly **10** 265 (0 below) and **0** entries are top-level tuples. Among terms drawn ≥2× in a 266 graph, **0** abbreviated terms violate len≥10 ∧ ¬tuple. Abbreviated terms with 267 graph-occurrence 1 (7 800) all reach ≥2 once the sequent is counted. 268 269 **Controlled probes (crafted theories, live server; see QUERIES.log):** 270 - `'12345678'` (10 chars, ×2) abbreviated; `'1234567'` (9, ×2) not ⇒ boundary = 10. 271 - `'ABCDEFGH'`/`'onceonly12'` (×1) not abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (×2) is; 272 a 42-char constant (×1) not abbreviated ⇒ occ ≥ 2 is a hard gate at any length. 273 - `<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, ×2) not abbreviated while sibling `h('longarg123')` 274 (×2) is ⇒ tuple exclusion + bottom-up nesting. 275 276 **Residual (sufficiency):** for **AC/DH-operator** sub-terms (`^ * ⊕ ++ inv em 277 hp pmult`) tamarin counts occurrences over the *normalised* (AC-flattened, 278 DH-normal-form) term, not the surface rendering, so the same term can be 279 abbreviated in one proof state and not another (`em(hp($A),hp($B))` inline in 280 state `56eeb42d`, `EM1` in `54eb2e2c`). 93 % of apparent exceptions are such 281 AC/DH cases; the rest are validation artifacts from expanding shared 282 abbreviations before counting. Outside AC/DH terms the rule is exact. 283 284 The crate `abbrev::select` implements this rule (`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, 285 `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2`, tuple-excluded, bottom-up); tests reproduce every probe. 286 Byte-parity of the selection set is claimed for non-AC/DH terms and gated by the 287 caller supplying the full-system term multiset. 288 289 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 290 ## 6. What the crate reproduces vs. gaps 291 292 Reproduced & byte-tested against captured payloads: 293 - DOT document assembly: both headers, block/whitespace rule, node lines 294 (record/ellipse/plain), edge lines with ports & attrs, cluster subgraphs, 295 legend sink-block + invis edges, empty graph. 296 VALIDATION: a parser for this dialect ingests each captured payload into the 297 crate's model; re-serializing reproduces the original bytes for **all 14705 298 DOT payloads in the corpus (14705/14705 byte-exact)**, incl. every clustered 299 and every largest graph. See `graph-clean/tests/roundtrip.rs`. 300 - Abbreviation naming (prefix derivation incl. operator map), per-prefix 301 numbering over a supplied order, and legend-table HTML (incl. the 65-space 302 hang indent and HTML escaping). 303 - Abbreviation SELECTION rule (§5c, REPORT2.md): renderLen ≥ 10 ∧ occ ≥ 2 ∧ 304 ¬tuple, bottom-up. Three necessary gates corpus-exact over 97 538 legend 305 entries (0 counterexamples) and each confirmed by a controlled live probe; 306 implemented as `abbrev::select` with tests. Exact for non-AC/DH terms. 307 - Cluster/compact trigger rule (role ≠ Undefined). 308 309 Documented gaps (need the GPL solver or an unavailable backend): 310 - JSON graph backend format (unavailable / not in corpus). 311 - Abbreviation of AC/DH sub-terms (§5c residual): occurrence is counted over the 312 solver's normalised (AC-flattened, DH-normal-form) term, not the surface 313 rendering — not derivable from output alone (~93% of §5c exceptions). 314 - Canonical per-prefix numbering tie-break (§5b); empty-prefix (numeric-constant) 315 start index. 316 - Term line-wrapping inside record labels and the color hashes (§3a, §4). 317 - The constraint-system → graph compression content (§4). 318

1	# REPORT2 — the abbreviation SELECTION rule (BEHAVIOR.md §5c gap)
2	
3	Clean-room continuation of `graphdot`. Everything below traces to observed
4	oracle output: the captured DOT corpus (`oracle/dot_corpus/`, 12 022 unique
5	payloads), the captured crawl manifests (`oracle/captured_responses/`, 81
6	theories; the `main/proof` json giving the full sequent paired 1:1 with each
7	`interactive-graph-def` DOT), and controlled probes of the black-box server
8	(`hs_server` binary, `--port=` form). No tamarin-prover source was read.
9	
10	## Result in one line
11	
12	> A sub-term `t` of a constraint system is abbreviated in the graph **iff**
13	> (1) its rendered length >= **10**, (2) it occurs >= **2** times in the system,
14	> and (3) it is not a tuple `<...>`. Naming is bottom-up (nested eligible
15	> sub-terms are named first).
16	
17	The prior named puzzle -- why `sign(...)` at 3 occurrences is abbreviated while
18	`KDF(z)` at 4 is not -- is resolved: the gate is rendered **string length**, not
19	structural size or occurrence count. `sign(<...>,...)` is >=10 chars; `KDF(z)`
20	is 6. The earlier investigator measured *size* and *occurrence* and so could not
21	separate them.
22	
23	## Evidence
24	
25	### (1) Length >= 10 -- necessary, corpus-exact
26	Over **all 97 538** legend expansions in **11 564** graphs (every legend in the
27	corpus), the minimum fully-expanded rendered length is **exactly 10**; **zero**
28	are shorter. Histogram floor: len10=7397, len11=3071, len12=3776, ... nothing at
29	5-9. Length is measured on tamarin's own rendering *including* surface
30	decorations -- quotes, `~`/`$` sigils (a bare `pk(PL1)` is only 7 chars but is
31	abbreviated because it *expands* to >=10; the decision is on the full expansion).
32	
33	Controlled probe (`LenTest.spthy`): a rule whose one node emits
34	`Keep(~x, '12345678', '12345678', '1234567', '1234567')`. Observed graph:
35	`Keep( ~x, 2, 2, '1234567', '1234567' )` with legend `2 = '12345678'`.
36	`'12345678'` = 10 chars -> abbreviated; `'1234567'` = 9 chars, **same occurrence
37	count** -> not. The boundary is exactly 10.
38	
39	### (2) Occurrence >= 2 -- necessary, hard gate independent of length
40	Structural per-graph analysis of 5 725 graphs (156 602 distinct candidate
41	sub-terms): among terms drawn >=2 times, **every** abbreviated term satisfies
42	len>=10 AND not-tuple -- **0** counterexamples. The 7 800 abbreviated terms the
43	graph-only occurrence "misses" all have graph-occurrence 1: they occur once in
44	the *drawn* graph but >=2 in the full system (the sequent's goals/formulas). This
45	is why terms such as `SI3 = sign(~y, k)` appear in a legend while being drawn
46	**nowhere** in the graph -- the abbreviation universe is the whole constraint
47	system, not the drawn nodes. Pairing each DOT with its `main/proof` json sequent
48	recovers those second occurrences.
49	
50	Controlled probe (`OccTest.spthy`): `'ABCDEFGH'` (10 chars, x1) and
51	`'onceonly12'` (12 chars, x1) are **not** abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (12, x2)
52	**is** (`TW1`). Probe (`LongOnce.spthy`): a **42-char** constant appearing once
53	is **not** abbreviated, while a 12-char constant appearing twice is. So there is
54	no "very long => abbreviate at 1 occurrence" branch -- occ >= 2 is a hard gate.
55	
56	### (3) Tuples are never abbreviated -- necessary, corpus-exact
57	**0 of 97 538** legend expansions is a top-level tuple. Only function
58	applications, exponentiations, AC-operator terms (`* (+) ++`), and atoms (long
59	constants/variables) are abbreviated. Controlled probe (`TupTest.spthy`):
60	`<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, x2) stays inline, while the sibling
61	`h('longarg123')` (x2) is abbreviated to `H1` with `LO1 = 'longarg123'`,
62	`H1 = h(LO1)` -- confirming the tuple exclusion and **bottom-up nesting** (inner
63	`'longarg123'` named before the enclosing `h(...)`), and the prefix rule
64	(`lo`->LO, `h`->H).
65	
66	## Accuracy and the residual
67	
68	The three gates are **necessary** with 0 corpus counterexamples (length, tuple)
69	/ 0 counterexamples among drawn->=2 terms (occurrence). For **sufficiency** --
70	is every (len>=10 AND occ>=2 AND not-tuple) term abbreviated? -- the controlled
71	probes say yes for ordinary terms, and the corpus agrees except for one
72	characterized residual:
73	
74	* **93.3%** of apparent sufficiency-exceptions occur in graphs using an **AC/DH
75	  operator** (`^ * (+) ++ inv em hp pmult`). Example: in state `56eeb42d`,
76	  `em(hp($A), hp($B))` (18 chars, drawn 7x) is *not* abbreviated, yet in a later
77	  state `54eb2e2c` the *same* term *is* (`EM1`). The difference is how DH
78	  exponentiation groups the term: tamarin counts occurrences over the
79	  **normalised** (AC-flattened, DH-normal-form) term, which a surface count does
80	  not model. This is solver-normalisation-dependent and cannot be derived from
81	  the rendering alone.
82	
83	* The remaining **~7%** are terms nested inside a *shared* abbreviated term
84	  (e.g. `pk(~ska.3)` inside one `sign(...)` threaded through an edge and drawn as
85	  `SI` in two facts). These are **measurement artifacts** of validating by
86	  *expanding* abbreviations before counting: expansion turns one edge-shared
87	  occurrence into several. The occurrence gate counts occurrences in the actual
88	  constraint system (an edge-shared message counts once), so these are not true
89	  rule violations -- a direct search finds **no** graph where such a term appears
90	  literally >=2x and is left un-abbreviated.
91	
92	Net: outside AC/DH-normalised sub-terms the rule is exact (confirmed by
93	controlled probe and by the AC-free portion of the corpus); the one honest gap
94	is the AC/DH occurrence-over-normal-form count.
95	
96	## What was implemented (`graph-clean`)
97	
98	`abbrev::select(roots) -> Vec<Term>` implements the rule: enumerate every
99	non-tuple sub-term of `roots` with `render_len() >= 10`, keep those occurring
100	>= 2 times, return them shortest-first (bottom-up). Thresholds are exposed as
101	`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and via `select_with(roots,min_len,
102	min_occ)` for probing. `roots` must be the full multiset of constraint-system
103	terms (graph node-fact arguments **and** sequent terms) because occurrence is a
104	whole-system count. Feeding the result through the existing `Abbreviator`
105	reproduces the legend end-to-end.
106	
107	`term::Term` gained `is_tuple()` and `render_len()` (Unicode-scalar count of the
108	full rendering, matching the observed 10-char boundary).
109	
110	Tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs`, all green) reproduce each controlled probe:
111	`select_length_boundary_is_ten`,
112	`select_requires_two_occurrences_even_for_long_terms`,
113	`select_never_abbreviates_a_tuple`, `select_then_name_reproduces_probe_legend`,
114	`select_counts_nested_occurrences`, `select_with_custom_thresholds`. Full suite
115	is 24 tests incl. the 12 022-payload byte-exact roundtrip.
116	
117	## Minor open detail
118	A numeric-only constant (`'12345678'`, no alphabetic chars) gets an **empty
119	prefix**, so its abbreviation name is a bare number -- observed as `2` for the
120	first such entry. The starting index for empty-prefix names was not pinned down
121	(single observation); it does not affect the length/occurrence/tuple rule.
122

1	# Similarity audit — graphdot cluster (clean room vs GPL Haskell)
2	
3	Auditor role: SIMILARITY AUDITOR (may read both sides). Discipline:
4	abstraction–filtration–comparison. A match is a VIOLATION only if it is expression
5	that could not plausibly come from observing program OUTPUT: mirrored internal helper
6	decomposition where alternatives exist, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names,
7	comments echoing Haskell comments, algorithmic expression matching where the observed
8	behavior admits materially different implementations, or any content present in the
9	Haskell source but in NO observable output.
10	
11	## Sides compared
12	
13	CLEAN (reimplementation):
14	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/model.rs` — syntactic DOT graph model
15	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/dot.rs` — byte-exact DOT serializer
16	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/term.rs` — small term model for legend expansions
17	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/abbrev.rs` — prefix derivation, numbering, legend HTML
18	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/lib.rs` — module glue
19	
20	HASKELL (originals, GPL):
21	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs`
22	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Abbreviation.hs`
23	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/GraphRepr.hs`
24	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Simplification.hs`
25	- (secondary dirty-room Rust ports: `crates/tamarin-server/src/graph/{repr,abbreviation,simplify,options}.rs`,
26	  `handlers/dot.rs` — translations of the Haskell; used only to understand the mapping)
27	
28	## Output-observability check (evidence the compatibility content is genuinely observed)
29	
30	Grepped the captured DOT payloads at `oracle/captured_responses`
31	(→ `tamarin-rs/scripts/.web_hs_cache`, 81 payloads). Confirmed present in OUTPUT:
32	- compact header incl. `packmode="cluster"` (8 payloads); simple header `nodesep="0.3"`.
33	- legend `<TABLE … CELLPADDING="1">` (73 payloads); `role=` on record nodes (79 payloads).
34	- abbrev names as PREFIX+counter: `AE1 AM1 … FS… EX…`; the exact 65-space hang indent
35	  before continuation `<TR>`; HTML escaping `&/</>`; exp rendering `'g'^…`.
36	- `FS` (from `F_status`) and `AM` (from `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`) both appear as OUTPUT names.
37	
38	Consequence: every string constant and every naming/rendering behavior the clean side
39	reproduces is present in observed output, so it is compatibility content, not expression
40	copied from source.
41	
42	Grepped the clean side for distinctive Haskell internal identifiers
43	(`judgeTerm`, `getTermPrefix`, `abbreviateTerm`, `makeRecursive`, `prefixMap`, `allNames`,
44	`relativeOccs`, `termWeight`, `topoSort`, `roleColor`, `simpleHash`, `generateValue`,
45	`renderBalanced`, `scaleIndent`, `dotNodeCompact`, `mergeLessEdges`, `tryHideNode`,
46	`compressSystem`, `transitiveReduction`, …): NONE present.
47	
48	## VIOLATIONS
49	
50	None. No finding survives filtration as expression that could not have come from
51	observation. No redo instructions are issued.
52	
53	## Per-module analysis
54	
55	### model.rs vs GraphRepr.hs — CLEARED (different abstraction level)
56	The clean model is a *syntactic* DOT model: `Header{Simple,Compact}`,
57	`Stmt{Node,Edge,Cluster,RankBlock}`, `NodeKind{Record,Ellipse,Plain}`,
58	`Record{columns,fillcolor,fontcolor,role}`, `Cell{port,text}`, `Ellipse`, `EndPoint`,
59	`Cluster{label,color,body}`, `RankBlock{rank,body}`. The Haskell `GraphRepr` is a
60	*semantic* model: `Node{nNodeId,nNodeType}`, `NodeType{SystemNode ru, UnsolvedActionNode,
61	LastActionAtom, MissingNode}`, `Edge{SystemEdge,LessEdge,UnsolvedChain}`,
62	`Cluster{cName,cNodes,cEdges}`. The two carry different data (records/ellipses/attrs vs
63	rule instances / premise-conclusion indices). No shared internal names, no mirrored
64	decomposition. Fields present are dictated by what the DOT output contains. Not a violation.
65	
66	- `Role::UNDEFINED = "Undefined"` (model.rs:29) mirrors Haskell's
67	  `fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)` (Dot.hs:243) only because `role="Undefined"`
68	  is emitted into the DOT output (verified in 79 payloads). Sentinel derived from output.
69	- `infer_header` = "compact iff any node has a non-Undefined role" (model.rs:56-64).
70	  Haskell's actual trigger is `null grClusters` (Dot.hs:503). These are different internal
71	  predicates that happen to coincide behaviorally (clusters are built only from role-bearing
72	  nodes). The clean side chose a *different* expression of the same observable correlation.
73	  Not a violation.
74	
75	### dot.rs vs Dot.hs — CLEARED (output strings + independent structure)
76	- `header_lines` Simple/Compact attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) are identical in content
77	  and order to `setDefaultAttributes` / `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` (Dot.hs:130-161).
78	  These are exact bytes emitted to output (verified) and their order is output-determined.
79	  Compatibility content. Critically, the Haskell per-attribute explanatory comments
80	  ("Slight increase to space out the graph", "Combine parallel edges", etc.) are NOT
81	  reproduced. Not a violation.
82	- Record/edge/endpoint/cluster/rankblock serialization (dot.rs:80-175) is plain Rust
83	  string formatting. Haskell builds via a monadic `Text.Dot` DSL (`D.record`, `D.node`,
84	  `D.edge`, StateT/ReaderT). Entirely different implementation strategy; the emitted bytes
85	  match because bytes are the observed contract. The `render_block` "blank line before `}`"
86	  rule (dot.rs:26-36) is reverse-engineered from output formatting. Not a violation.
87	- `subgraph "cluster_…"` prefix (dot.rs:165) is DOT output syntax present in payloads.
88	
89	### term.rs vs Term.LTerm view (used in Abbreviation.hs) — CLEARED (renderings are output)
90	- `Term{Fresh,Pub,Msg,Const,Pair,Exp,Ac,App}` and its renderings `~n / $n / n / 'n' /
91	  <a, b, c> / base^e / (a op b) / f(a,b)` (term.rs:144-165) all reproduce surface syntax
92	  visible in node labels and legend expansions (verified `'g'^…`). The Ac/Exp-vs-App split
93	  is forced by observed output (AC operators and exp render infix; ordinary functions render
94	  prefix). Not a violation.
95	- `Ac` operator table `mult→* , union→++ , xor→⊕` and `root_symbol_name` mapping
96	  `mult/union/xor/exp/pair` (term.rs:57-83) use lowercase logical names, whereas Haskell
97	  derives the prefix from `show o` = `Mult/Union/Xor` and `BC.unpack s` = `exp`
98	  (Abbreviation.hs:109-116). Different source expression; identical observable prefixes
99	  (MU/UN/XO/EX seen in output). Not a violation.
100	- `for_each_subterm` is fully recursive pre-order (term.rs:104-118); Haskell `getSubTerms`
101	  is one level only (`t : ts`, Abbreviation.hs:213-215). Divergent, not mirrored.
102	
103	### abbrev.rs vs Abbreviation.hs — CLEARED (closest calls documented below)
104	- Legend HTML (`TABLE_OPEN`, `legend_row`, `legend_html`, `escape_expansion`,
105	  abbrev.rs:19-151) reproduces exact output bytes: table/cell tags, `<FONT COLOR="#000000">`,
106	  the `=` cell, `&/</>` escaping, and the 65-space hang indent equal to
107	  `len(TABLE_OPEN)`. All verified in captured payloads; the indent was measured from output,
108	  not taken from the Haskell (whose layout comes from the `Text.Dot` pretty-printer, no such
109	  constant in source). Compatibility content. Not a violation.
110	- `Abbreviator` numbering: per-prefix `1,2,3,…` with dedup by full rendering
111	  (abbrev.rs:63-75). Names ARE the output (PREFIX+counter, verified). The clean side does
112	  NOT reproduce Haskell's collision-avoidance against `allNames`
113	  (Abbreviation.hs:135-137, 220-225), soft-limit / always-abbrev-weight
114	  (Abbreviation.hs:178), weight formula `judgeTerm` (Abbreviation.hs:83-101), or the
115	  legend `topoSortAbbrevs` ordering (Dot.hs:456-474) — all source-only machinery is absent.
116	  Not a violation.
117	- `select` (abbrev.rs:158-176) is an explicitly-labeled best-effort heuristic (count
118	  occurrences, size threshold, sort ascending by size). It is materially different from the
119	  Haskell greedy max-weight algorithm with subterm-occurrence decrementing
120	  (`computeAbbreviations`, Abbreviation.hs:166-188), and even sorts in the opposite
121	  direction. Not a violation.
122	
123	#### Closest call (considered, CLEARED): prefix derivation
124	- Clean `prefix_for_symbol` (abbrev.rs:33-39):
125	  `chars.filter(is_ascii_alphabetic).take(2).flat_map(to_uppercase)`.
126	- Haskell `getTermPrefix` (Abbreviation.hs:107):
127	  `map toUpper . take (aoPrefixLength) . filter isAlpha`.
128	- The pipeline is the same three operations in the same order (filter-alpha → take-2 →
129	  uppercase). FILTRATION: the observed output forces exactly this semantics and even pins
130	  the operation ORDER. `F_status → FS` (present in output) can only arise from
131	  filter-then-take (take-then-filter would yield `F`); `h2 → H`, `KDF → KD`,
132	  `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID → AM`, `senc → SE` fix "first two alphabetic characters, uppercased,
133	  non-letters skipped, length 2." There is no materially different implementation of that
134	  forced behavior. Per protocol this is "identical behavior at the idea level" / "structure
135	  forced by the output format," not expression. CLEARED — no redo. (If a future reviewer
136	  wants extra distance anyway, the behavioral redo would be: derive the two-letter uppercase
137	  tag by scanning characters and collecting letters directly, rather than as a
138	  filter/take/map chain — but this is not required, as the current code is output-forced.)
139	
140	### Simplification.hs / GraphRepr.hs clustering — NO CLEAN COUNTERPART
141	The clean cluster has no analog to `compressSystem`, `dropEntailedOrdConstraints`,
142	`transitiveReduction`, `tryHideNodeId` (Simplification.hs) or `addCluster`,
143	`groupNodesByRole`, `findConnectedComponents`, `extractBaseName`, `roleColor`/`simpleHash`
144	color hashing (GraphRepr.hs / Dot.hs:525-544). The clean side takes cluster label and color
145	as pre-computed inputs (model.rs:202-210). The one piece of source-only, non-observable
146	expression that would be a red flag if copied — the `simpleHash`/`generateValue`/`roleColor`
147	constants (31, 7, HSV 0.75/0.85, alpha 0.3) — is entirely ABSENT from the clean side.
148	Strong positive signal for non-access.
149	
150	## Conclusion
151	The clean-room modules are an independent reimplementation. Every similarity to the Haskell
152	traces to observable OUTPUT (exact DOT/HTML strings, abbrev names, surface-syntax renderings)
153	or to behavior that the output forces. No mirrored internal decomposition, no shared internal
154	names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only machinery (weight/greedy abbreviation,
155	collision avoidance, topo-sort ordering, color hashing, system simplification/clustering)
156	appears on the clean side. VERDICT: PASS. No redo instructions.
157	
158	## Round 2 incremental audit
159	
160	Scope: the abbreviation SELECTION policy added this round —
161	`abbrev::select` / `select_with` / `MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10` / `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and the
162	tuple-exclusion + bottom-up-ordering it uses (`abbrev.rs:157-210`), the supporting
163	`term.rs` additions (`render_len`, `is_tuple`, `for_each_subterm`, `size`,
164	`term.rs:91-134`), and the `select_*` tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs:238-320`).
165	Haskell original: the term-judging / selection machinery in
166	`Graph/Abbreviation.hs` — `judgeTerm` (83-101), `computeAbbreviations`/`go`
167	(166-231), `getFactTerms`/`getSubTerms` (209-215), `defaultAbbreviationOptions`
168	(66-72). Key question posed: does the clean rule reproduce the Haskell EXPRESSION
169	(constants, weight formula, decomposition) or only observable BEHAVIOR?
170	
171	### Side-by-side (abstraction–filtration)
172	
173	| aspect | Haskell (Abbreviation.hs) | Clean (`select`) | observable? | disposition |
174	|--------|---------------------------|------------------|-------------|-------------|
175	| length gate ≥10 | `judgeTerm … \| termWeight < 10 = -1` where `termWeight = length $ render $ prettyLNTerm replacedTerm` | `render_len() >= MIN_ABBREV_LEN` (10) | YES — corpus floor is exactly 10 over 97 538 legends, and probe `'12345678'`(10)→abbrev vs `'1234567'`(9)→not | FILTERED (output-forced boundary) |
176	| occurrence gate ≥2 | `\| relativeOccs <= 1 = -1` | count over `roots` `>= MIN_ABBREV_OCC` (2) | YES — probe: 42-char×1 not abbreviated, 12-char×2 abbreviated | FILTERED |
177	| tuple/pair exclusion | `getFactTerms = filter (not . isPair) …` | skip `t.is_tuple()` | YES — 0/97 538 legends is a top-level tuple; probe left an 18-char tuple×2 inline | FILTERED |
178	| length measured on the **rendered string** | `length $ render $ prettyLNTerm` (of the abbrev-**applied** term) | `render_full().chars().count()` (fully **expanded**, no abbrev applied) | YES (measurable per legend) — and the two measures *differ* in the iterative case | FILTERED / DIVERGENT |
179	| product weight `relativeOccs * termWeight` | present (the ranking value) | **absent** | NO — a non-observable internal ordering weight | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
180	| greedy max-weight pick + per-subterm occurrence decrement loop (`go`) | present | **absent** (flat filter, no iteration) | NO | NOT reproduced |
181	| soft-limit `aoAbbrevsSoftLimit=10` / `aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight=30` cutoff | present (178) | **absent** | NO — source-only constants | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
182	| `relativeOccs` legend-only special case (`occs==1 && legendOccs==[1] → 0`) | present (97-101) | **absent** | NO | NOT reproduced |
183	| subterm enumeration | one level: `getSubTerms t = t : ts` (215) | **fully recursive** pre-order `for_each_subterm` | — | DIVERGENT (opposite depth), not mirrored |
184	| ordering of survivors | decreasing weight (`sortOn Down`, greedy-first) | **ascending** `render_len` (shortest/inner first) | numbering only partly observable | OPPOSITE direction — not mirrored |
185	| occurrence universe | graph `grNodes`/cluster nodes (192-205) | whole constraint system (`roots` = node-fact args **and** sequent terms), derived because legends carry terms drawn nowhere | observation-derived | DIVERGENT, not mirrored |
186	
187	### Assessment of the key question
188	The clean rule reproduces the observable BEHAVIOR, not the Haskell EXPRESSION.
189	The three gates it keeps (len≥10, occ≥2, not-tuple) are each independently
190	witnessed by corpus statistics and by controlled boundary probes
191	(QUERIES.log / BEHAVIOR §5c), so reproducing them — including the two literal
192	constants 10 and 2, which the corpus pins directly — is compatibility content.
193	The predicate "len≥10 AND occ≥2 AND not-tuple" is the minimal expression of that
194	observed selection behavior and admits no materially different implementation of
195	the predicate itself; it is output-forced, not copied.
196	
197	Decisively, the entire non-observable apparatus that surrounds those gates in the
198	source is ABSENT on the clean side: the product weight formula, the greedy
199	max-weight/decrement iteration, the soft-limit + always-abbrev-weight cutoff
200	(constants 10/30), the `relativeOccs` legend special-case, and the one-level
201	`getSubTerms`. Where the clean side had latitude it chose *differently* from the
202	source — full-recursive enumeration vs one-level, ascending-length ordering vs
203	greedy decreasing-weight, fully-expanded length vs abbrev-applied length, a
204	whole-system occurrence universe vs `grNodes`. These divergences are affirmative
205	evidence of derivation from observation rather than from the source.
206	
207	No shared internal names (clean `select`/`select_with`/`render_len`/`is_tuple`/
208	`for_each_subterm`/`size` vs HS `judgeTerm`/`computeAbbreviations`/`termWeight`/
209	`relativeOccs`/`getSubTerms`/`getFactTerms`/`aoAbbrevsSoftLimit`). No echoed
210	comments — the clean comments cite the clean room's own corpus/probe evidence,
211	whereas the HS comments describe the weight design and its `cleandot.py` origin.
212	
213	### VIOLATIONS (Round 2)
214	None. No finding survives filtration as non-observable expression. No redo
215	instructions. VERDICT: PASS.

import re,glob,collections
CORP='/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus'
def records(dot):
    for m in re.finditer(r'shape="record",label="(\{.*?\})",fillcolor=', dot):
        yield m.group(1)
def split_top(s,sep):
    out=[];d=0;cur='';esc=False
    for c in s:
        if esc:cur+=c;esc=False;continue
        if c=='\\':cur+=c;esc=True;continue
        if c=='{':d+=1;cur+=c
        elif c=='}':d-=1;cur+=c
        elif c==sep and d==0:out.append(cur);cur=''
        else:cur+=c
    out.append(cur);return out
ngroups=collections.Counter()
info_pos=collections.Counter()
info_present=0; total=0
for f in glob.glob(CORP+'/*.dot'):
    dot=open(f).read()
    for lbl in records(dot):
        inner=lbl[1:-1]
        groups=split_top(inner,'|')
        ngroups[len(groups)]+=1
        total+=1
        # which group holds the info cell (#t : Rule) ?
        pos=None
        for gi,g in enumerate(groups):
            cells=split_top(g[1:-1],'|')
            for c in cells:
                if re.search(r'<n\d+> #[\w.]+ : ', c):
                    pos=gi
        if pos is not None:
            info_present+=1
            info_pos[(len(groups),pos)]+=1
print('records:',total,' with info cell:',info_present, f'({100*info_present/total:.1f}%)')
print('group-count distribution:',dict(ngroups))
print('(#groups, info-group-index) distribution:')
for k,v in sorted(info_pos.items()): print('  ',k,v)

## 6. System → graph mapping (§ Round-3 item 4)

From paired `main/proof` sequents and `interactive-graph-def` graphs, the
structural mapping a proof state yields (each point traces to §3d/§3f and the
content census; the *content* — which nodes/edges exist — is a solver GAP):
- a **rule instance** `#t : Rule[actions]` → a `record` node with groups
  `{premises}|{info}|{conclusions}` (empty prem/concl dropped, info always kept);
- an **intruder-knowledge fact** → a gray `!KU( m ) @ #t` ellipse;
- a **protocol action/event** → a darkblue `Fact @ #t` ellipse;
- a **compressed intruder rule** → an uncolored `#t : rule` ellipse (§4 compression);
- an **unresolved node** referenced by an open premise → an `invtrapezium`
  `(#var, idx)` (§3d);
- **edges** connect conclusion-port → premise-port (structural bold w10 [gray50]),
  intruder deduction (red dashed), message (gray30), temporal order
  (blue3/black dashed), etc. — the finite §3c vocabulary;
- ids from §3e; header from §4 (role); legend from §5.

`graph-clean::generate` implements this over an independent input model
(`System`/`GraphNode`/`RuleInstance`/`SysEdge`), taking node/edge lists, colors,
and cell text as INPUTS. `tests/generate_tests.rs` reproduces a live source-case
graph (`nsl_invtrap.dot`) **byte-exact** end-to-end.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. Simplification / abbreviation options (§ Round-3 item 5)

The UI's "Graph simplification off/L1/L2/L3" menu and abbreviation toggle are
cookie-driven and appended by the JS as graph-URL **query params** (mined from
`/static/js/tamarin-prover-ui.js`, confirmed by live diffs — QUERIES.log):

| param              | UI sends when                    | observed effect                          |
|--------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `simplification=N` | always (N=level, default 2)      | selects level; **N alone changed no probed graph** (L1≡L2≡L3) |
| `uncompact=`       | level 0 only                     | node COMPACTION off (lone intruder rule → full record vs ellipse) |
| `uncompress=`      | level 0 only                     | system COMPRESSION off (Fresh sources + intruder rules re-expand to records) |
| `unabbreviate=`    | abbreviate cookie off            | abbreviation off (terms inlined, legend block omitted) |
| `no-auto-sources=` | auto-sources cookie off          | disables auto-source precomputation      |
| `clustering=true`  | clustering cookie on             | forces role clustering                   |

The default (no params, as the corpus was crawled) = compressed + compacted +
abbreviated (≡ L1/L2/L3). `graph-clean::options::Options` models the knobs and
reproduces the exact query string; the abbreviation on/off transform is applied
directly (drop legend / inline). **GAPS:** the L1/L2/L3 distinction (unobserved on
every probed graph) and the *content* of compress/compact (a solver transform —
the caller supplies the already-(un)compressed node set).

(`scratchpad/serve.sh`) execs the same black-box binary as `oracle/hs_server.sh`,
only with the `--port=` form the binary requires. Mining scripts and probe
transcript: `workspace/QUERIES.log`; observed spec: `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`.

## Results by spec item

### 1. Record-cell term rendering + line wrapping (BEHAVIOR §3f)
- **Group structure** (byte-verified, 160 409 records): label = the *non-empty*
  groups of `[premises, info, conclusions]`; the info cell (`#t : Rule[…]`) is
  present in 100 %; empty premise/conclusion groups are dropped.
- **Spacing** (mined): facts pad `Name( a, b )` / `Name( )`; functions do not,
  `f(a, b)`; tuples `<a, b>`. Info cell `#t : Rule[acts]` or bare `#t : Rule`.
- **Escaping**: `< > { } |` backslash-escaped inside record labels.
- **Wrapping**: the continuation indent is a ` ` run equal to the column of
  the broken group's **first element** (after `( ` / `<` / `[`), verified across
  188 192 wrapped cells; segments joined by `\l` with a trailing `\l`. The *break
  decision* (which line-width, which group) is the solver's pretty-printer — first
  physical-line length ranges 5..87, so it is not a flat-width wrap and is a
  documented GAP. Implemented in `src/render.rs`; `render` reproduces an observed
  wrapped cell (`Corrupt_SessionKey[…]`) byte-exact and every unwrapped fact.

### 2. Shape vocabulary (BEHAVIOR §3d)
Corpus (all 12 022 dot + 81 manifests) uses only record/ellipse/plain — because
the crawl is all *solved* proof graphs. Live probing (case-distinction and partial
proof graphs of two theories) adds **`invtrapezium`**: a `(#var, idx)` placeholder
for an unresolved node referenced by an open premise, fed by the conclusion that
must satisfy it. **`trapezium`** (spec-named dual) was NOT observed in any probe or
the corpus. Added `NodeKind::Shaped` / `Shaped::invtrapezium`; the serializer emits
any shape string. Fixtures `nsl_invtrap.dot`, `invtrap_compressed.dot` round-trip.

### 3. Node-id / port allocation (BEHAVIOR §3e)
One global counter, ids in emission order (== file order); a record takes one id
per cell (premises→info→conclusions) then the node id; other kinds take one id.
**Verified 12 022/12 022** by re-deriving every id from scratch — first in Python
(`mine_ids.py`), then in Rust via the crate's own `NodeIdAllocator`
(`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`, 12 022/12 022).

### 4. Graph content — system → graph mapping (BEHAVIOR §6)
Characterized from paired json/dot + a corpus content census (11 fixed edge
styles; ellipse colors none/gray/darkblue; ellipse kinds !KU / action / compressed
rule). Implemented `generate` over an independent input model (`System`,
`GraphNode`, `RuleInstance`, `SysEdge`, `EdgeStyle`, `EndRef`) that assembles the
exact DOT structure — records with the group-drop rule, the ellipse kinds, the
invtrapezium, the edge vocabulary, id allocation, header inference, and the legend
sink-block. `tests/generate_tests.rs` builds a `System` and reproduces the live
`nsl_invtrap.dot` **byte-exact** end-to-end. The *content* (which nodes/edges a raw
constraint system yields, the compression) needs the solver → GAP; this layer
takes the node/edge set as input.

### 5. Simplification levels (BEHAVIOR §7)
The "Graph simplification off/L1/L2/L3" menu + abbreviation toggle are cookie-driven
graph-URL query params (mined from the served UI JS, confirmed by live diffs):
`simplification=N` (default 2, always), `uncompact=`/`uncompress=` (level 0),
`unabbreviate=`, `no-auto-sources=`, `clustering=true`. Observed transforms:
**compression** hides Fresh sources and collapses intruder rules to `!KU`/isend
ellipses; **compaction** draws a lone intruder rule as one ellipse; **abbreviation**
adds the legend and names sub-terms. Turning each off re-expands / inlines. The
default (no params, as crawled) ≡ L1/L2/L3 = compressed+compacted+abbreviated;
`simplification=1/2/3` alone changed **no** probed graph (L1/L2/L3 distinction is a
GAP), and the compress/compact *content* is a solver GAP. `src/options.rs` models
the knobs and reproduces the exact query string.

## Validation
`cargo test`: 44 tests green (11 unit + 16 abbrev + 4 generate + 2 alloc + 10
roundtrip + 1 doc). Corpus gates (opt-in `GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS`): round-trip
12 022/12 022 byte-exact; allocator 12 022/12 022 consistent. Deps: std +
serde/serde_json only.

## Net
Serialization (Round 1) + abbreviation selection (Round 2) + GENERATION (this
round: id allocation, record-cell rendering, the invtrapezium shape, the system→
graph mapping, and the simplification-option model) are all implemented and tested
against observed output. The honest residuals are solver/pretty-printer internals
not derivable from output: the record-cell wrap *decision*, the L1/L2/L3 split, the
compression *content*, the color hashes, and the unobserved `trapezium`.
